title: Upcycled "Milk Carton" Side Shields to make Splash-proof Safety Glasses
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"575ae","text":"This procedure describes how to make \"side shields\" to regular eye glasses in order to make them suitable for EHS safety requirements when working with chemical solutions that pose splash hazards to the eyes. This protocol uses materials that would otherwise be thrown away, and upcycles other items (glasses) to become more useful and safe for laboratory environments. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"6sol5","text":"- Hard but somewhat flexible plastic material (e.g. plastic milk carton, hard clear plastic from a salad box, etc).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cb9c6","text":"- Scissors","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"coaea","text":"- Something to make holes. This could be an awl, a small screwdriver, a pointy screw, hole punches of small-medium sizes, etc","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5mdn3","text":"-Optional: Marker and paper to make a template ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Source your materials. Whatever you use should be wipe-able - plastic milk cartons are a good material for this. Other options are laminated paper, and other hard but flexible plastics that would otherwise be thrown away.

Find a hole punch or similar item that is the appropriate width for the size and placement of the holes for the side shields, as determined in steps 3+ 4 above. (e.g. I used a very tiny hole punch, then used a screw to widen the hole. ) Punch holes in the premarked spots from Step 3. Use scissors to make holes wider/longer as needed, but be careful not to cut too big.
Examine your side shield-less glasses that you have on hand. Cut side shield shape that will work with the shape of your glasses. You can make a "template" using paper and a marker as shown below
Hold your cut out plastic pieces next to the glasses. Mark where each piece need to have holes cut in order to remain on the glasses snugly. (this can also be done on the template first)

Determine how big of a hole should be cut. You will need to cut two holes per side shield piece. The plastic material you chose should be somewhat flexible, so it should be able to get forced open a bit with the action of putting it on the glasses. You do not want to side shields to be *too loose* otherwise they are not safe! We do not want the plastic to slide around, it needs to be snug and secure to protect your eyes from chemical splashes.
Slide each piece onto the glasses
Male sure that the plastic slides OVER the edge of the glasses rather than under - this is for extra safety.
creator: Elena Carlson
